title: Family investors flock to old-economy businesses amid AI disruption fears
body:
In a surprising shift, family investors are increasingly turning their attention to traditional industries such as car dealerships and fisheries, seeking to avoid the disruption caused by the rapid rise of artificial intelligence. These old-economy businesses may not have the glamour of cutting-edge tech startups, but they offer a unique combination of stability and cash flow that is proving attractive to family offices. As the investment landscape continues to evolve, it appears that some of the wealthiest investors are taking a step back from the hype surrounding AI and focusing on more tried-and-true sectors.

This trend is driven in part by the growing uncertainty surrounding the impact of AI on various industries. As automation and machine learning technologies advance, many investors are becoming wary of putting their money into sectors that may be heavily disrupted in the near future. In contrast, businesses like car dealerships and fisheries have been around for decades, and their fundamental models are less likely to be upended by technological innovation. Additionally, these industries often have significant barriers to entry, making it more difficult for new competitors to emerge and challenge established players.

Family offices, which manage the wealth of high-net-worth individuals and families, are particularly drawn to the steady cash flow generated by these traditional businesses. Car dealerships, for example, typically have a consistent stream of revenue from sales, service, and financing activities. Fisheries, meanwhile, can provide a stable source of income through the sale of fresh seafood and other marine products. These predictable revenue streams are highly valued by family offices, which often prioritize preserving and growing their wealth over chasing high-risk, high-reward investments.

The appeal of old-economy businesses extends beyond their financial characteristics, however. Many family investors are also motivated by a desire to support industries that have been integral to their communities for generations. By investing in local car dealerships or fisheries, family offices can help preserve jobs and contribute to the economic vitality of their regions. This approach not only generates returns but also allows investors to make a positive impact on the people and places that matter most to them.

As the investment landscape continues to shift, it will be interesting to see whether this trend towards old-economy businesses gains momentum. Some experts predict that the rise of AI will ultimately lead to a reassessment of what constitutes a "traditional" industry, as even seemingly stable sectors are forced to adapt to new technologies. Nevertheless, for family offices seeking stability and cash flow, car dealerships and fisheries are likely to remain attractive options for the foreseeable future.

Looking ahead, it is clear that family investors will need to navigate an increasingly complex and rapidly evolving investment landscape. As AI continues to disrupt industries and create new opportunities, it will be essential for family offices to balance their desire for stability with the need to stay ahead of the curve. By embracing a diversified approach that combines traditional businesses with more innovative investments, family investors can position themselves for long-term success and help shape the future of the global economy. With their focus on old-economy businesses, family offices are taking a pragmatic and forward-thinking approach to wealth management, one that prioritizes resilience and adaptability in an uncertain world.
